reasoning: Tank is cycling predictably: zero ammonia/nitrite, nitrate at 80ppm confirms active nitrification. All parameters green—temp stable, pH drifting slightly alkaline (normal as minerals accumulate), TDS in comfort zone. This is a healthy Day 20 snapshot.
recommended_actions: Continue daily observation of pH trend; may warrant a 20-25% water change if it climbs past 7.2.
Monitor TDS weekly; when it hits 250ppm, perform partial water change.
If shrimp present, watch for feeding/behavior changes as indicator of bioload stability.
suggested_actuator_actions: None required. Tank stable—no heater/chiller/air adjustment needed.

temperature.note: Stable, well within range. Slight downtrend (-0.22°F/24h) is normal.

ph.note: Just below target floor (6.5). 24h mean 6.499 suggests drift, not spike. Test logged 6.4 at 23:06 confirms trend.

tds.note: Mid-range, healthy. +5ppm/24h reflects nitrate accumulation in active cycle—expected.

reasoning: pH is riding the edge of target—not emergency, but the 24h trend (mean 6.499, now 6.48) shows a gentle downward drift that warrants attention before livestock arrive. Nitrate at 80ppm and climbing is normal for day 20 cycling; temperature and TDS are solid.
recommended_actions: Monitor pH every 6–12 hours through day 22. Flag if it drops below 6.2 or shows acceleration.
Plan a 25–30% water change by day 22–23 to arrest pH drift and manage nitrate (expect 100–120ppm by then).
Investigate ESP32 WiFi instability reported in journal—may affect future alert reliability.
suggested_actuator_actions: No immediate action needed. Tank is stable enough to hold at current parameters.
When actuators online: consider pH buffer dosing if drift continues below 6.3, but water change is preferred solution.

temperature.note: Stable, within target. Slight cooling trend (-0.46°F/24hr) is normal.

ph.note: Just below target floor (6.5). Mean 6.499 shows tight clustering; trending downward slightly (-0.03 pH/24hr). Cycle still running—acid production expected.

tds.note: Healthy mid-range. Nitrate at 80ppm (from test) explains TDS; accumulation expected as cycle matures.

reasoning: pH is marginally low but stable—not in free fall. Day 20 of cycling: nitrification is producing nitric acid, which gently depresses pH. This is normal and temporary. Watch the 48-hour trend; if it drops below 6.0 or continues this trajectory, intervene with small water change.
recommended_actions: Observe pH for next 48 hours. Flag if it falls below 6.0 or shows acceleration downward.
No immediate action needed—chemistry is acceptable for cycling phase.
Prepare small (20-25%) water change as contingency if pH dips further.
Monitor ESP32 WiFi stability; elevated post failures could affect data fidelity.
suggested_actuator_actions: Hold on heater and chiller—temperature is stable and well-managed.
No pH buffer or adjustment yet—let cycling complete naturally; shrimp can tolerate 6.48 briefly.
